points.text: Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-8102 (NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader) — Cardiovascular disease risk reduction — Interim GFORCE-1 trial data showed profound CRP reductions in subjects with elevated CVD risk; expanded readout anticipated H2 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-2359 (GSPT1-directed molecular glue degrader) — Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) — Positive interim Phase 1/2 data in combination with AR inhibitor for patients with AR mutations; Phase 2 study with apalutamide planned Q3 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-6160 (VAV1-directed molecular glue degrader) — Immune-mediated diseases — Advancing toward Novartis-led multiple Phase 2 study initiations in H2 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Cyclin E1-directed molecular glue degrader — Oncology — Preclinical data presented at AACR demonstrated superior selectivity and reduced off-target activity vs CDK2 inhibitors; IND submission anticipated H2 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics (Nasdaq: GLUE), a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing novel molecular glue degrader (MGD)-based medicines, reported Q1 2026 financial results with $671 million in cash and securities to support operations into 2029.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-8102 — Cardiovascular disease / Gout / Hidradenitis suppurativa — Company expects to initiate Phase 2 studies in elevated atherosclerotic risk (H2 2026), gout flares (Q4 2026/Q1 2027), and hidradenitis suppurativa (H1 2027).
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-2359 — Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer — Phase 2 study evaluating combination with apalutamide in patients with AR mutations planned for Q3 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-6160 — Immune-mediated diseases — VAV1-directed MGD; advancing toward Novartis-led multiple Phase 2 study initiations in 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Cyclin E1-directed MGD — Oncology (preclinical) — Novel degrader with superior selectivity vs CDK2 inhibitors; IND submission anticipated H2 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader (MGD) pipeline advancement — MRT-8102, MRT-2359, MRT-6160, Cyclin E1-MGD — $671M cash runway to 2029; three clinical programs approaching Phase 2 initiation.
Novartis — Collaboration on molecular glue degraders — MRT-6160 (VAV1-directed MGD) — Executing multiple Phase 2 study initiations in immune-mediated diseases in 2026.

MRT-8102 — Mechanism: NEK7-directed molecular glue degrader — Phase: 1/2 (GFORCE-1) — Population: Subjects with elevated CVD risk — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Profound CRP reductions; rapid, deep, and durable systemic inflammation reduction; expanded readout expected H2 2026.

MRT-2359 — Mechanism: GSPT1-directed molecular glue degrader — Phase: 1/2 — Population: mCRPC patients with AR mutations — Comparator: AR inhibitor combination — Results: Positive interim data; Phase 2 with apalutamide planned Q3 2026.

Cyclin E1-directed MGD — Mechanism: Molecular glue degrader targeting Cyclin E1 — Phase: Preclinical — Comparator: CDK2 inhibitors — Results: Superior selectivity and reduced off-target activity; IND submission H2 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics / Novartis — Partnership execution — MRT-6160 (VAV1-directed MGD) — Novartis to initiate multiple Phase 2 studies in immune-mediated diseases in H2 2026.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Financial position — Molecular glue pipeline — $671 million in cash, equivalents, and securities as of Q1 2026 to fund operations through 2029.
